---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: ea06b99b90d7df43564d7c3fd4001bc9eb985eeb
findings:
  - { id: judged-todo-free-rendering-body-prose, kind: judged, text: "ac-1 claims that supplying --problem/--outcome (and, by ac-2, the TTY interview) makes 'every section the class template declares render TODO-free — never the `TODO: replace with the real problem statement before accept` / `TODO: design notes.` placeholders the unflagged path always emitted.' But --problem/--outcome and the interview answers only flow into the frontmatter scalars `problem: { text: ... }` / `outcome: { text: ... }` via ScaffoldData.Problem/Outcome. The canonical templates (internal/designscaffold/templates/feature.md and story.md) also contain markdown BODY sections `## Problem`, `## Outcome` (and `## Ac 1`) whose text is the literal, untemplated string `TODO: design notes.`. Render() executes the template verbatim and nothing rewrites the body, so even on the flagged/interview paths the rendered scaffold still contains `TODO: design notes.` in its `## Problem` and `## Outcome` prose sections. The `TODO: design notes.` placeholder the AC promises will 'never' survive is exactly the one that does — the scaffold does not render TODO-free. Mechanical boundary checks pass because the flags correctly reach ScaffoldData; the divergence is that 'section' in the AC covers the prose body sections the template hardcodes, which the statement-sourcing wiring never reaches. (confidence 0.62)" }
digest: sha256:a507d077dd1b56749979ea8e9adea1845a8f94fc8061949c4adaf0ea037d085d
integrity: sha256:3d7ded2fe808f031a69c9ba7c40ef1ab2df7cb33e6ae4cda629e6ec6199e6ba7
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "I've found a clear semantic deviation. Let me verify the rendering path once more — confirming nothing post-processes the body prose.\n\nThe templates confirm it: the frontmatter `problem:`/`outcome:` scalars are templated from `.Problem`/`.Outcome`, but the markdown body sections `## Problem`, `## Outcome` (and `## Ac 1`) carry a **literal, untemplated** `TODO: design notes.` that no flag or interview answer touches.\n\nac-1 explicitly promises that the `--problem`/`--outcome` path makes \"every section the class template declares render TODO-free — never the `TODO: replace with the real problem statement before accept` / **`TODO: design notes.`** placeholders.\" The implementation only sources the frontmatter scalars; the prose `## Problem`/`## Outcome` body sections still render `TODO: design notes.`, so the scaffold is not TODO-free as the AC states, even on the flagged (and interview) paths.\n\n```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"todo-free-rendering-body-prose\",\"text\":\"ac-1 claims that supplying --problem/--outcome (and, by ac-2, the TTY interview) makes 'every section the class template declares render TODO-free — never the `TODO: replace with the real problem statement before accept` / `TODO: design notes.` placeholders the unflagged path always emitted.' But --problem/--outcome and the interview answers only flow into the frontmatter scalars `problem: { text: ... }` / `outcome: { text: ... }` via ScaffoldData.Problem/Outcome. The canonical templates (internal/designscaffold/templates/feature.md and story.md) also contain markdown BODY sections `## Problem`, `## Outcome` (and `## Ac 1`) whose text is the literal, untemplated string `TODO: design notes.`. Render() executes the template verbatim and nothing rewrites the body, so even on the flagged/interview paths the rendered scaffold still contains `TODO: design notes.` in its `## Problem` and `## Outcome` prose sections. The `TODO: design notes.` placeholder the AC promises will 'never' survive is exactly the one that does — the scaffold does not render TODO-free. Mechanical boundary checks pass because the flags correctly reach ScaffoldData; the divergence is that 'section' in the AC covers the prose body sections the template hardcodes, which the statement-sourcing wiring never reaches.\",\"confidence\":0.62}]}\n```" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/cli-creation@ea06b99b90d7df43564d7c3fd4001bc9eb985eeb, spec/cli-creation@e1cd2d1f957a200804b97a78829482d1ca8b57f9], digest: sha256:a507d077dd1b56749979ea8e9adea1845a8f94fc8061949c4adaf0ea037d085d, integrity: sha256:3d7ded2fe808f031a69c9ba7c40ef1ab2df7cb33e6ae4cda629e6ec6199e6ba7, model: sha256:a8b2e3c1eb4defba461355077a2b82ae13db212beaad6e78eab05db51e5b6721 }

# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-todo-free-rendering-body-prose** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: ac-1 claims that supplying --problem/--outcome (and, by ac-2, the TTY interview) makes 'every section the class template declares render TODO-free — never the `TODO: replace with the real problem statement before accept` / `TODO: design notes.` placeholders the unflagged path always emitted.' But --problem/--outcome and the interview answers only flow into the frontmatter scalars `problem: { text: ... }` / `outcome: { text: ... }` via ScaffoldData.Problem/Outcome. The canonical templates (internal/designscaffold/templates/feature.md and story.md) also contain markdown BODY sections `## Problem`, `## Outcome` (and `## Ac 1`) whose text is the literal, untemplated string `TODO: design notes.`. Render() executes the template verbatim and nothing rewrites the body, so even on the flagged/interview paths the rendered scaffold still contains `TODO: design notes.` in its `## Problem` and `## Outcome` prose sections. The `TODO: design notes.` placeholder the AC promises will 'never' survive is exactly the one that does — the scaffold does not render TODO-free. Mechanical boundary checks pass because the flags correctly reach ScaffoldData; the divergence is that 'section' in the AC covers the prose body sections the template hardcodes, which the statement-sourcing wiring never reaches. (confidence 0.62)

### Candidates awaiting reaffirmation

(none)
